R'hllor (the Lord of Light, the Red God)
Faith of the Seven (the Seven who are one — Maid, Mother, Warrior, Smith, Crone, Father, Stranger)

Ancient books of Asshai (referenced by Melisandre — contain the Azor Ahai prophecy)

mentions.raw_name: Azor Ahai prophecy (warrior draws burning sword from fire after a long summer; Melisandre declares Stannis is Azor Ahai reborn)
R'hllor's apparent protection of Melisandre from poison (Cressen's attempt failed)
Seeing visions in flames (Ser Axell claims to have seen dancing maidens; Melisandre reportedly teaches this)
Thoros of Myr's flaming sword trick (at Joffrey's name day tourney — wildfire-based, no true magic)
Lightbringer (prophesied Red Sword of Heroes; the actual sword's flames die quickly)
The forging of Lightbringer legend (three attempts; final success through Nissa Nissa's sacrifice)
War of the Five Kings (implied — four kings named: Joffrey, Stannis, Renly, Robb Stark)
The fight at the sept (Rambtons vs. queen's men — four queen's men killed, one Rambton son killed)
Prince of the Narrow Sea (Salladhor Saan, self-styled)
King of the Andals, the Rhoynar, and the First Men (Stannis's claimed title)
Lord of the Seven Kingdoms (Stannis's claimed title)
Castellan of Dragonstone (Ser Axell served for ten years)
Lord of Storm's End (Steffon Baratheon's title)
The Stranger depicted "more animal than human" in the carved statue
Davos's sigil: a black ship with an onion on its sail
House Baratheon sigil: a crowned stag
The fiery heart badge (worn by Devan and Bryen Farring — new Stannis/R'hllor emblem)
